user_prompt: Plot two triangles on a coordinate plane to show they're congruent by SSS. Draw triangle ABC with A at (1, 1), B at (4, 1), and C at (4, 5). Then draw triangle DEF with D at (-1, -1), E at (-4, -1), and F at (-4, -5). Label all six points with their coordinates. Draw dashed tick marks to show AB = DE, BC = EF, and AC = DF. Also draw a dashed arrow showing that triangle ABC can be mapped onto triangle DEF by a reflection across the origin or a 180-degree rotation.
